back_translation: 'My spirit is shattered; my days are extinguished; the grave is waiting for me.
Surely mockers surround me, and my eye must gaze on their provocation.
Lay down a pledge for me with Yourself; who else will be a guarantor for me?
For You have closed their minds to understanding; therefore You will not exalt them.
The one who betrays friends for gain—the eyes of his children will grow dim.
You have made me a byword among the peoples, a man in whose face people spit.
My eyes have grown dim from grief, and my whole body is but a shadow.
The upright are appalled at this, and the innocent rise up against the godless.
Yet the righteous holds to his way, and the one with clean hands grows stronger.
But all of you, come back and try again, for I will not find a wise man among you.
My days have passed, my plans are broken off—even the desires of my heart.
They turn night into day, treating light as near in the face of darkness.
If I hope for Sheol as my home, and spread my bed in the darkness,
and say to the pit of decay, 'You are my father,' and to the worm, 'My mother' or 'My sister,'
where then is my hope? Who can see any hope for me?
Will it go down to the gates of Sheol? Will we descend together into the dust?'
